title: ASML Q3 2025 Quarterly Results
content: VELDHOVEN, Netherlands — ASML Holding N.V. today announced its results for the third quarter of 2025. Net sales for Q3 2025 were €7.5 billion, compared to €6.7 billion in Q3 2024. EUV revenue was €4.6 billion, Non-EUV (deep-UV and other) was €2.9 billion. Gross profit was €3.81 billion, representing a gross margin of 50.8%. Net income was €2.08 billion. Net bookings (order intake) in Q3 were €2.63 billion, of which EUV bookings were €1.5 billion. Three High-NA EUV machines were shipped in Q3, at approximately €350 million each. ASML CEO Christophe Fouquet said: 'Semiconductor demand is being driven by AI infrastructure build-out at an unprecedented pace. Our EUV and High-NA EUV systems are indispensable for manufacturing the most advanced chips in the world, and we remain the sole supplier of this technology.' ASML guided Q4 2025 net sales of €8.8-9.2 billion. The company reaffirmed its 2025 guidance of €30-35 billion in net sales and a gross margin of approximately 51-53%. ASML has a near-monopoly in EUV lithography, with over 200 EUV systems installed at customer sites globally. The company employs approximately 44,000 people, primarily in the Netherlands. ASML paid an interim dividend of €1.52 per share in August 2025 and expects to pay a total 2025 dividend of approximately €6.40 per share.
